source_pr.title: planner: prepared plan cache support cached plan with placeholder in limit clause
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->
support limit in plan cache, the execution plan may vary with the limit offset and count, so if we want to use cache, we'd better encode the offset and count into plan cache key to ensure the plan's correctness
Issue Number:  #40219 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Prepared plan cache could incorrectly reuse a cached plan for statements with `LIMIT ?` / `LIMIT ?, ?` placeholders even when the limit/offset parameter values change, because the limit values were not encoded into the plan cache key.
failure_signature: Incorrect plan-cache hit/miss for prepared statements with LIMIT placeholders (e.g. `@@last_plan_from_cache` stays 1 even after changing LIMIT/OFFSET parameter values).
root_cause_explanation: The plan-cache key did not account for the `LIMIT`/`OFFSET` placeholder values extracted from the prepared statement AST, so different executions of the same prepared statement could share the same cache key even when the limit/offset values change. This can lead to an incorrect cache hit and reuse an unsuitable plan. The fix extracts limit offset/count values from the prepared AST and encodes them into the plan cache key, and adds unit tests (e.g. `TestPlanCacheWithLimit`) to verify cache hit/miss behavior when changing limit parameters.
fix_pattern: Include extracted LIMIT/OFFSET placeholder values in the prepared plan cache key so different limit parameters do not incorrectly share cached plans; add unit tests to verify `@@last_plan_from_cache` behavior.

evidence: evidence: {"prepare stmt from 'delete from t order by a limit ?'", []int{1}}, (planner/core/plan_cache_test.go)

files: executor/prepared.go, executor/seqtest/prepared_test.go, planner/core/logical_plan_builder.go, planner/core/plan_cache.go, planner/core/plan_cache_lru.go, planner/core/plan_cache_lru_test.go, planner/core/plan_cache_test.go, planner/core/plan_cache_utils.go, planner/core/plan_cache_utils_test.go, planner/core/plan_cacheable_checker.go, planner/core/plan_cacheable_checker_test.go, server/driver_tidb.go, session/session.go, sessionctx/context.go
